title: What was your first thought after this scene
display_title: What was your first thought after this scene
wikitext:
[]: What was your first thought after this scene?             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Tue Sep 19, 2006 11:07 pm Okay, I was watching Eva for the first time, and I had gotten to Instrumentality. I believe it was episode 25 when we heard this. Shinji: Don't leave me Misato: Don't ignore me Asuka: Don't kill me I still remember because I burst out laughing when I thouught it. The scene reminded me from the mantra of The Who's Tommy. See me Feel me Touch me Heal me... So, what exactly was the first thought through your mind when that happened.

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Tue Sep 19, 2006 11:08 pm Reminded me of In the Shadows by The Rasmus.

[]:             Quote Post by PilotIkari » Wed Sep 20, 2006 2:46 am Throughout the whole of Instrumentality I was reminded of Pink Floyd: The Wall. Especially Rei & Shinji's emotional barriers are very alike to Pink's "Wall" - Blocking everyone out and keeping them in.

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Wed Sep 20, 2006 4:25 am PilotIkari wrote: Throughout the whole of Instrumentality I was reminded of Pink Floyd: The Wall. Especially Rei & Shinji's emotional barriers are very alike to Pink's "Wall" - Blocking everyone out and keeping them in.

[]: Re: What was your first thought after this scene?             Quote Post by zeaven » Wed Sep 20, 2006 10:32 am CorporalChaos wrote: See me Feel me Touch me Heal me...

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Sep 20, 2006 4:35 pm The sub version, if I remember correctly, is a more curious and semi-accepting "What is this?". Spike Spencer came off to me as rather indignant about the whole instrumentality thing, but then again, I kinda feel Spike played Shinji with a bit more confidence than Shinji actually had...

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Wed Sep 20, 2006 4:40 pm i didnt feel anything. not as good as most other scenes

[]: Re: What was your first thought after this scene?             Quote Post by Karen_Leslie » Wed Sep 20, 2006 5:49 pm zeaven wrote: Nine Inch Nails - Eraser (Polite) wrote: Need you Dream you Find you Taste you Use you Scar you Fuck you Break you...

[]: Re: What was your first thought after this scene?             Quote Post by zeaven » Thu Sep 21, 2006 12:30 pm Karen_Leslie wrote: but mirrors the strong feeling of "WTF?" that the audience is feeling at that moment

[]:             Quote Post by Karen_Leslie » Thu Sep 21, 2006 4:39 pm I think it's more like, the people who were inclined to think it was all psychobabble anyway would interpret it that way. I can go "WTF???" and still realize that there's substance behind what's going on. There are many times in Eva where they use humor to reinforce a serious point, not undercut it. I don't see how getting some humor out of the fact that instrumentality is confusing to Shinji as well takes away from the point of the episode-- if he understood everything from the beginning, 25 & 26 would be unnessessary.

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Sat Sep 23, 2006 2:28 pm Karen_Leslie wrote: I think it's more like, the people who were inclined to think it was all psychobabble anyway would interpret it that way. I can go "WTF???" and still realize that there's substance behind what's going on. There are many times in Eva where they use humor to reinforce a serious point, not undercut it. I don't see how getting some humor out of the fact that instrumentality is confusing to Shinji as well takes away from the point of the episode-- if he understood everything from the beginning, 25 & 26 would be unnessessary.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Sat Sep 23, 2006 2:41 pm It further demonstrated his at the very least physical attraction to both Misato and Asuka? I dunno, I'm very tired and trying to think logically.

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Sat Sep 23, 2006 2:48 pm Good point, but who wouldn't be physically attracted to them?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Sep 23, 2006 3:17 pm Mundane wrote: Good point, but who wouldn't be physically attracted to them?

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Sat Sep 23, 2006 3:29 pm Touche salesman.

[]:             Quote Post by zeaven » Sat Sep 23, 2006 6:53 pm Also known as "Hey, the characters are now in a hot spring. We need filler between arriving and Misato talking to Asuka about her past. Aaaand... GO!" (zany hijinks ensue)

[]:             Quote Post by Karen_Leslie » Mon Sep 25, 2006 2:21 pm Mundane wrote: The scene at the springs enforced what serious point?

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon Sep 25, 2006 2:31 pm I was merely truing to grasp what the hell wwasd going on. I don't think I was making any comparisons.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Sep 25, 2006 4:52 pm Yes, I write a lot. The only thing I remember thinking is once I watched EoE and Shinji starts yelling at that in Asuka in his mind/Instrumenality was: Holy Shit, it IS concurrent! The fact Asuka says "Don't kill me!" in 25 and Shinji strangles her in 26'...Think about it. :) (Though it could always be a refrence to her "mama" as well.) While acutally watching that and 26 however, I remember feeling extermely confused (the typical WTF is going on here response) but at the same time, I'm probably one of the 10 other people in the world who greatly enjoyed those episodes and the (TV) ending. As I haven't watched the dub of that or the movies except for a few selected scenes, I can't really comment. The only thing I can say is Ogata Megumi has that screaming down. ; Sudden thought: If Anno and GAINAX got death threats (people are fucking stupid, it's just a show and it's not MEANT to mean only one thing! ) for the TV ending, wonder if anything crazy like that would happen for the new 4 movies project. (And, no, I don't think EoE or Death & Rebirth was made as "revenge". Seems like it was somewhat planned in advance, what with a white naked Rei in 23', Eva Unit 01 with wings in the OP, Misato and Ritsuko's death in 25, the Next Episode Preview for "Air" after 24' (from the tv-released version of End of Eva or whatever the hell it's acutal name is.) etc...) About episodes 25 and 26, Shinji's Rant at the end is always fun. XD and yes, Karen_Leslie, good metaphor. I personally loved the humor of the show, and I guess there are a few times it's meant to "foreshadow" things in a way. I'm not smart enough to come up with any of the connections I mention, but one thing I read somewhere was the somewhat-funny scene of Misato letting Ritsuko know Shinji would move in with her ("It's not like I'm gonna "put the moves" on a kid!") foreshadows that "irony bomb" in episode 23/23'. Also, according to something else I read, the humor was meant to be taken in an uncomfortable sense, but that aspect I've never really seen. I just take the humor as what it is: meant to be fucking funny. And just in general (this part I did come up with on my own, yay), any sexual or otherwise awkwardness, humorus or not, between Shinji and the three females seems to show the fact that he's attracted to each of them to some degree but doesn't know what to do about that. From my understanding: Rei due to her mysteriousness, Misato due to the motherly/big sister attitude and semi-shoutacon feelings she seems to feel towards him (on occasion), and Asuka due to her sexually teasing him. Feel free to contribute and add on to what I wrote, just don't make any major agurements with me yet as I'm still trying to figure out just what the hell I'm trying to say. ;

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Sep 25, 2006 5:17 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: The fact Asuka says "Don't kill me!" in 25 and Shinji strangles her in 26'...Think about it. :) (Though it could always be a refrence to her "mama" as well.)
